{"step_by_step_thinking": "The question asks which vitamin deficiency most commonly affects the gingiva. Document [4] explicitly mentions 'Scurvy: Vitamin C deficiency can produce very tender bleeding gingiva.' Document [8] lists 'gingival inflammation' as a symptom of vitamin C deficiency. Document [13] states that 'bleeding gums and gingivitis are suggestive of vitamin C deficiency.' Additionally, Document [10] confirms that 'Vitamin C deficiency manifests as fatigue, muscle cramping, gingival inflammation, and gingival hypertrophy in some cases.' While vitamin B deficiencies are associated with angular cheilosis and periodontal diseases (Document [0]), the specific and most commonly cited link to gingival bleeding and inflammation is with vitamin C deficiency.
